name: Netherlands Antilles (1986-2010)

colors.symbolism: love and the bond with the Netherlands
the Caribbean Sea and the bond with the Netherlands

description: A white field with a horizontal blue band and a vertical red band crossing at the centre. On the red band, five white five-pointed stars arranged in a circle.
history: Adopted on 1 January 1986 when Aruba seceded from the Netherlands Antilles to become a separate constituent country of the Kingdom of the Netherlands. The 1959 six-star design was modified by removing one star, leaving five for the remaining islands: Bonaire, Curaçao, Saba, Sint Eustatius, and Sint Maarten. The flag flew until 10 October 2010, when the Netherlands Antilles was dissolved: Curaçao and Sint Maarten became separate constituent countries, while Bonaire, Saba, and Sint Eustatius became special municipalities (the BES islands) of the Netherlands proper.

features.symbolism: a horizontal blue band crossed by a vertical red band on a white field — combining the colours of the Netherlands flag in a distinctive arrangement

features.role: Five stars on the central red band
features.symbolism: the five remaining island territories of the Netherlands Antilles after Aruba's secession: Bonaire, Curaçao, Saba, Sint Eustatius, and Sint Maarten
